new_classes.label_en: Other non-sparkling fermented beverages and mixtures (CN 2206 00 39)
new_classes.label_de: Andere, nicht schäumende gegorene Getränke und Mischungen (KN 2206 00 39)
new_classes.definition_en: Other fermented beverages and mixtures of fermented beverages and non-alcoholic beverages falling under CN heading 2206, other than piquette (2206 00 10) and sparkling beverages (2206 00 31), presented as non-sparkling. Includes the products described in the HS Explanatory Note to heading 2206, second paragraph, points (1) to (10), such as cider, perry, mead, saké, and similar fermented beverages, that are not sparkling.

coverage_explanation:
The axioms capture the three key discriminating criteria for CN 2206 00 39 within the 2206 hierarchy: (1) membership in the OtherFermentedBeverages2206 parent class (fermented beverage or mixture thereof, not classifiable elsewhere); (2) non-sparkling character (complement of SparklingWineQuality, distinguishing from 2206 00 31); (3) not produced by the piquette process (distinguishing from 2206 00 10). 

Coverage is partial (0.75) because: (a) The HS Explanatory Note second paragraph (1)–(10) enumerates specific product types (cider, perry, mead, saké, kvass, fermented milk-based beverages, etc.) but these are not individually axiomatised as quality or process classes in the current TBox — doing so would require introducing ten new named quality/process classes not yet present; (b) the 'other' residual nature of 2206 00 39 (i.e., not otherwise specified) is partly captured by the complement restrictions but cannot be fully formalised as a closed-world enumeration within OWL open-world semantics; (c) a separate 'non-sparkling pressure quality' class could in principle be defined (< 1 bar excess pressure at 20°C) but is not yet present in the TBox, and no dedicated property for this threshold exists under 2206.
